meta_description: Learn how to deployment workflow and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Deployment Workflow to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to build a professional deployment workflow and overcome a lack of portfolio. An Intermediate-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop letting a lack of portfolio stall your momentum. By automating your deployment workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code, you transform raw technical capability into a live, verifiable SaaS asset. This creates an immediate proof-of-work layer that convinces investors and users alike that you are a serious, ship-first founder.
content.intro:
You have the vision and the technical chops, but your GitHub profile looks like a desert. In the brutal economy of SaaS, ideas are cheap; verified production-grade deployments are the only currency that matters. Founders who cannot demonstrate a live, breathing product lose the trust of stakeholders before the first meeting even begins.

But here is the secret: you don't need a decade of experience to build a portfolio. You need a high-velocity deployment pipeline that proves you can ship. By leveraging Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for implementation, we are going to build a professional, automated workflow that turns your MVP into a showcase of engineering excellence.

steps.title: Define Infrastructure as Code
steps.description: Establish a reproducible environment. We use Antigravity to define our cloud footprint, ensuring our deployment is immutable and scalable from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Terraform configuration for a production-ready AWS ECS Fargate cluster with a private VPC, load balancer, and RDS PostgreSQL instance.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize infrastructure boilerplate using the generated Terraform files'

steps.title: Configure CI/CD Pipelines
steps.description: Automate the transition from code-push to production. This proves your ability to manage professional release cycles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, unit tests, and triggers a rolling update deployment to ECS upon merge to main.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Apply the GitHub Actions workflow and configure repository secrets'

steps.title: Implement Monitoring and Observability
steps.description: A portfolio is incomplete without proof of operation. Add logging and metrics to show you build for reliability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up CloudWatch logs and custom metrics for a FastAPI application running on ECS, including a dashboard definition.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Integrate CloudWatch SDK into the application and deploy the dashboard configuration'

steps.title: Containerize the SaaS Core
steps.description: Standardize your runtime environment. This ensures your app runs identically on your machine and in the cloud.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a multi-stage Dockerfile for a Python/FastAPI backend, optimizing for small image size and fast build times.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create the Dockerfile and verify build locally via docker build -t my-saas .'

steps.title: Automate Domain and SSL Provisioning
steps.description: Finalize your professional footprint by securing your endpoints, demonstrating attention to production security.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure AWS Certificate Manager and Route 53 records for a custom domain via Terraform.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Apply SSL/TLS configuration and finalize DNS routing'

pro_tips: Always treat your infrastructure repository as a product; documentation and READMEs are your first line of defense against investor skepticism.
Use ephemeral environments for every pull request; it shows you understand how to manage high-velocity team collaboration.
Include a 'status page' in your portfolio that monitors your live deployment; real-time uptime data is better than any screenshot.
Automate your database migrations within the CI/CD pipeline rather than running them manually to demonstrate production safety.
Always set up cost-alerting thresholds on your cloud account—it shows you are a responsible steward of capital.
content.next_steps: Once your pipeline is live, document the architecture in a 'Systems Case Study' blog post to accompany your portfolio, highlighting the specific challenges you solved during deployment.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped with the workflow of a senior architect. Don't let your code sit on a local drive when it could be powering your next venture. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
